stories.title: Antonelli's Historic Championship Lead — Mercedes' Intra-Team Battle Heats Up

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
19 years old. Championship leader. TWO wins in a row.

Kimi Antonelli is the youngest F1 title leader in history (19y 216d).

Teammate Russell is 9 points behind. Wolff is managing the battle.

Something has to give at Miami. #F1Fanrecap #F1

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Mercedes hasn't lost a race start all year — they've had FOUR front-row lockouts.

But neither Antonelli nor Russell has led Lap 1.

That's their only weakness. And they're working on it before Miami.

The W17 might be perfect. #F1Fanrecap #MercedesAMGF1

article.headline: Antonelli's Historic Championship Lead: The Youngest-Ever F1 Title Contender and Mercedes' Intra-Team Battle

image_concepts.label: Image 1A
text_overlay: 19 YEARS OLD / CHAMPIONSHIP LEADER" in Bebas Neue gold

stories.title: Ferrari's Major Miami Upgrade Package — "Reset" Moment for SF-26

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Ferrari tested their Miami upgrade package at Monza in secret.

New floor. Macarena rear wing. Hamilton + Leclerc evaluated the full aero evolution.

Vasseur calls Miami the "reset" moment for the championship.

45 points down. Not done. #F1Fanrecap #Ferrari

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The Macarena rear wing. The new floor. A Monza filming day test.

Ferrari is going all-in at Miami. If this package works, the title fight is ON.

45 points behind Mercedes. Reset button incoming. #F1Fanrecap #ScuderiaFerrari

article.headline: Ferrari's Miami Upgrade Gamble: New Floor, Macarena Wing, and a Championship Reset

image_concepts.label: Image 2A
text_overlay: FERRARI RESET / MIAMI UPGRADE PACKAGE" in Bebas Neue red/white

stories.title: FIA Confirms Rule Changes for Miami — 2026 Regulations Refined

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
F1's 2026 rules just changed — AGAIN. All stakeholders agreed on April 21.

Super clipping: 250kW → 350kW.
Recharge limit: 8MJ → 7MJ.
New start detection system debuts Miami.

The sport is self-correcting in real time. #F1Fanrecap #F1

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
The FIA trialing a "low power start detection system" at Miami.

Translation: Verstappen complained about cars losing power at starts — the FIA listened.

New era. New rules. New drama. #F1Fanrecap #F12026

article.headline: FIA Agrees 2026 Rule Changes for Miami: Super Clipping Boost, Energy Limits, and a New Start System

image_concepts.label: Image 3A
text_overlay: NEW RULES / MIAMI 2026" in Bebas Neue white

stories.title: McLaren Miami Charge + Red Bull's Technical Reshuffle

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
McLaren's first real upgrades arrive at Miami. Piastri took P2 in Japan — now the MCL40 gets its first major aero package.

Red Bull is 119 points behind Mercedes after just 3 races.

Midfield is about to look completely different. #F1Fanrecap #McLaren

article.headline: McLaren's Miami Charge and Red Bull's Technical Rebuild: The Midfield Story of 2026

image_concepts.label: Image 4A
text_overlay: MCLAREN UPGRADES / MIAMI INCOMING" in Bebas Neue orange/white

stories.title: Miami Grand Prix Preview — Sprint Weekend, Schedule, What to Watch

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Miami Grand Prix — May 3, 2026. Sprint weekend. New 2026 rules. Ferrari upgrades. McLaren's first real package.

Mercedes have won every race. That's about to be tested.

Sprint qualifying Friday May 1 at 4:30 PM EDT. Get ready. #F1Fanrecap #MiamiGP

article.headline: Miami Grand Prix 2026 Preview: Sprint Format, New Rules, and F1's Season-Defining Moment
